name: Idel-Ural State (1917–1918)

colors.symbolism: Islam and the Volga Tatars / Bashkirs

description: Three horizontal stripes — green, white, red.
history: Flag of the Idel-Ural State (Volga-Ural state), a short-lived Tatar-Bashkir-led entity proclaimed in March 1918 in the wake of the Russian Revolution, encompassing the Tatar, Bashkir and Chuvash territories of the middle Volga and southern Urals. Suppressed by the Bolsheviks within months. The flag remains a symbol for Tatar and Volga-region cultural movements.
